docs.title: QDD Arm — Sim-to-Real Documentation
docs.text:
The complete operators guide for the 6-DOF QDD arm sim-to-real stack: capture VLA datasets in sim and on real hardware, fine-tune GR00T N1.6 with domain randomisation, evaluate the policy in Isaac Lab and on the physical arm, and (if needed) co-train on a sim-real mix to close the gap.

Your browser does not support the video tag.

  • Overview

    Architecture (host + Docker, ZMQ glue), prerequisites, quick-start commands, hardware-free validation test, project layout, configuration reference, troubleshooting.

  • Architecture & Setup

    Detailed addverb_isaaclab/ layout, scene/USD/camera config, rendering & latency knobs, install steps, full pipeline.

  • Isaac Lab Notes

    Hybrid host + container architecture, GPU/X11 passthrough, Docker build internals.

  • Stage 1a — Data Collection (Sim)

    Hardware leader → Isaac Sim follower with domain randomisation. Calibration, the 3-terminal flow, Parquet + JPEG output schema, multi-episode strategies, troubleshooting.

  • Stage 1b — Real-Hardware Data Collection

    Same dataset format captured on the physical arm with two USB cameras (top + wrist; no sim involvement). Used for Stage 4 demos and the real-side of Stage 5 co-training.

  • Stages 2–5 — Sim-to-Real Training

    Domain-randomised dataset → GR00T N1.6 fine-tune → sim eval → real eval → co-training, with the full training command adapted to our 7-DoF arm.

docs.title: Stage cheat sheet
docs.text: Stage What runs Key knob Pass criterion 1a — Sim capture scripts/launch_bridge.sh + scripts/launch_sim.sh agent_config.yaml → logging (rate, dataset_root) + dataset_config.yaml → task_ranges (prompts) + isaac_scene_config.yaml → domain_randomization (DR) 50–200 demos per task in addverb_sim_dataset/ 1b — Real capture addverb_arms/addverb_vla_datacollection/run_datacollect_docker.sh config_single.py 50–200 demos per task in addverb_single_arm_dataset/ 2 — Train gr00t/experiment/launch_finetune.py --base_model_path nvidia/GR00T-N1.6-3B, --max_steps 10000, --learning_rate 1e-4, --global_batch_size 32 Loss curve plateaus; checkpoint at 10 000 steps 3 — Sim eval lerobot_eval against Addverb-Teleop-Cam-v0-Eval --action_horizon 16, DR on ≥ 50–70 % success over ≥ 30 rollouts 4 — Real eval run_gr00t_server.py + real-arm client Same checkpoint as Stage 3 Acceptable real-world success rate 5 — Co-train Re-run Stage 2 on sim+real mix 14 : 1 to 20 : 1 sim : real ratio (see the doc) Closes the sim → real gap

docs.title: Conventions
docs.text:
• Joint vector is always 7 entries: [link_1..link_6] (rad) + slide_1 (m). slide_2 mirrors slide_1 automatically (env hook + USD axis flip). link_7 is welded as a PhysicsFixedJoint in the USD physics layer (the wrist flange is never actuated on hardware), so it has no DOF and never appears in the action / observation vectors.
  • Gripper kinematics is slider-crank, calibrated by a single reference angle physical_open_rad.
  • Single source of truth for launching is launch_config.yaml at the repo root. Both launch_bridge.sh and launch_sim.sh read it.
  • Dataset format is identical between sim and real captures (Parquet rows + per-episode JPEGs), so the two streams can be mixed for co-training (Stage 5).
  • Domain randomisation ranges live in isaac_scene_config.yaml → domain_randomization. Each block carries its own enabled: true|false flag (default false); only enabled blocks are wired as reset events at env build time.
  • Logging knobs (operational): rate, queue depth, debug-print frequency, output path → agent_config.yaml → logging. Dataset schema (image dims, jpeg quality, dof layout, task_ranges) → dataset_config.yaml. Task prompts come from dataset_config.yaml → task_ranges for sim captures, and from EPISODE_TASK_RANGES in addverb_vla_datacollection/config_single.py for hardware captures.

If you find a stale section, fix the source markdown and refresh. The sources are the same files engineers read in the repo:

docs.title: Addverb Arms → Isaac Sim: Teleoperation & Data Collection Bridge
docs.text:
This package uses the Addverb 6-DOF robotic arm as the physical leader in a sim-to-real teleoperation pipeline backed by Isaac Lab (Isaac Sim). The sim follower is mounted on a table top, a cube spawns in front of it, and the operator picks the cube up with the physical leader while RGB streams, joint positions and leader/follower commands are logged in the exact same Parquet + JPEG format used by addverb_vla_datacollection.

The Addverb arm has 6 DOF. The driver reports a 7-element joint vector where indices [0:6] are arm joints and index [6] is the gripper (same unit: radians).

flowchart TB
    subgraph SCENE["Isaac Lab scene"]
        direction TB
        TBL["Table<br/>(static cuboid, deterministic)"]
        ROBOT["Addverb 6-DOF arm + 1 prismatic gripper<br/>mounted on table top"]
        CUBE["5 cm red cube<br/>random (x,y) on table top<br/>per env reset"]
        EGO["camera_ego<br/>wrist-mounted RGB"]
        EXT["camera_external<br/>fixed 3/4 view RGB"]
        TBL --- ROBOT
        TBL --- CUBE
        ROBOT --- EGO
        TBL --- EXT
    end
    EGO -- live + dataset --> WC["WristCam viewport"]
    EXT -- live + dataset --> TC["TableCam viewport"]
    EGO -- 30 Hz JPEG --> DS[("SimDataLogger")]
    EXT -- 30 Hz JPEG --> DS
  • Table — static cuboid (configurable size/centre/colour in isaac_scene_config.yaml → table). The robot is mounted on the top surface; its base position is deterministic and never randomised.
  • Cube — 5 cm red cube that spawns at a random (x, y) on the table top each reset, inside the arm's reachable envelope. Spawn range and size live in isaac_scene_config.yaml → cube.
  • Cameras —
  • camera_ego — wrist-mounted on the end effector.
  • camera_external — fixed isometric ¾ view of the workspace. Both RGB streams are shown live in floating viewport windows (WristCam, TableCam) that open automatically when the agent starts, and are written into the dataset at 30 Hz.

docs.title: Step 1 — Isaac Lab (Sim PC only)
docs.text:
Follow the official Isaac Lab installation guide: https://isaac-sim.github.io/IsaacLab/main/source/setup/installation/index.html

This repo also ships a Docker image — see Docker (Sim PC) below for the supported path.

docs.title: Step 2 — Addverb pybind driver (Hardware PC)
docs.text:
cd addverb_arms/python_bindings
# Follow the build instructions in that directory (cmake + pip install)
pip install -e .

docs.title: Step 3 — Python dependencies
docs.text:
Both PCs: 

pip install pyzmq numpy PyYAML click

Hardware PC only — for the R/E keyboard listener in hardware_bridge_sender.py: 

pip install pynput      # X11 session; stdin fallback otherwise

Sim PC (in addition to Isaac Lab's own env): 

pip install torch opencv-python scipy pandas pyarrow

Or everything from the requirements file: 

pip install -r addverb_isaaclab/requirements.txt

docs.title: Step 4 — Robot USD asset
docs.text:
The robot is loaded from a pre-converted USD bundle that lives in the repo:

addverb_isaaclab/assets/usd/addverb_arm/
    addverb_gripper_urdf_2.usd            # top-level (robot articulation, variants)
    configuration/
        addverb_gripper_urdf_2_base.usd   # geometry (~63 MB)
        addverb_gripper_urdf_2_physics.usd # joints + drives + welds + slide axis flip
        addverb_gripper_urdf_2_sensor.usd
        materials/

run_sim.sh mounts assets/usd/ into the container read-write and the agent loads addverb_gripper_urdf_2.usd directly. There is no URDF→USD conversion step. To swap in a new robot, replace the bundle in place (the addverb_gripper_urdf_2.usd top-level uses relative refs into configuration/).

docs.title: Step 5 — Joint name verification
docs.text: Launch the env once and copy the names from the Isaac joint order: ... banner into config/agent_config.yaml → kinematics.isaac_joint_names.

docs.title: Configuration Files
docs.text: File Controls config/bridge_config.yaml CAN interface, leader gains, ZMQ port, loop Hz config/agent_config.yaml ZMQ address, kinematics (joint names/sign flips/offsets/limits/gripper), logging knobs (fps, dataset_root, debug rate, queue depth) config/isaac_scene_config.yaml Table, cube, robot mount, cameras, physics, rendering, domain randomisation config/dataset_config.yaml Dataset schema: dof layout, image dims, jpeg quality, task prompts (task_ranges), codebase/robot type config/calibration.yaml Auto-written by --calibrate; overlays offsets + gripper range

docs.title: Where do task prompts come from?
docs.text: The text written into each parquet row's task_prompt field is resolved from dataset_config.yaml → task_ranges. Each entry is [start_episode, end_episode, task_name, task_prompt]; at log time SimDataLogger._get_task_prompt(episode_num) finds the entry whose [start, end] range contains the active episode and stamps that prompt into both the row and meta/tasks.parquet. Edit task_ranges to attach different prompts to different episode-number ranges.

docs.title: Domain randomisation
docs.text:
isaac_scene_config.yaml → domain_randomization exposes per-knob enabled flags so each block can be turned on/off independently:

domain_randomization:
  camera_ego_focal_length:
    enabled: false
    range_mm: [12.0, 15.0]
  camera_external_pose:
    enabled: false
    pos_range: { x: [-0.02, 0.02], y: [-0.02, 0.02], z: [-0.01, 0.01] }
    rot_range: { roll: [-0.05, 0.05], pitch: [-0.05, 0.05], yaw: [-0.05, 0.05] }
  light_exposure:
    enabled: false
    range_ev: [-3.0, 1.0]

Each enabled block adds a corresponding EventTermCfg(mode="reset", ...) to AddverbEventCfg (via sim_agent/addverb_env_cfg.py), backed by the helper functions in sim_agent/mdp/resets.py (randomize_camera_focal_length, randomize_camera_pose, randomize_light_exposure). With every block disabled (the default) the env behaves identically to a no-DR run.

docs.title: Rendering / Latency Tuning
docs.text:
Teleop latency is dominated by sensor rendering. All rendering knobs live in one section of isaac_scene_config.yaml:

rendering:
  mode: "performance"                # "performance" | "balanced" | "quality"
  enable_translucency: false
  camera_update_period_s: 0.0333     # ≈ 30 Hz sensor update
  carb_settings: {}                  # raw carb overrides — use with caution

mode: "performance" already turns off RTX reflections / AO / DLSS and picks cheap defaults for shadows. enable_translucency is a first-class Isaac Lab attribute and safe to flip.

carb_settings is a raw pass-through — Isaac Lab validates keys against its known list and an unknown key aborts env construction (for example rtx.post.dlss.enabled is rejected on Isaac Lab 2.3). Only put a key in carb_settings after you've confirmed it exists on your Isaac Lab version.

The container mounts addverb_isaaclab/config/ as read-write, so edits on the host take effect at the next container launch without a rebuild.

If teleop feels sluggish, tune in this order: 1. Increase camera_update_period_s — biggest single win (30 Hz → 20 Hz → 15 Hz). 2. Drop camera_ego.width/height and camera_external.width/height (render cost scales quadratically). 3. Leave mode: "performance". 4. Raise physics.decimation (fewer renders per second at the cost of physics accuracy).

docs.title: Docker (recommended, Sim PC)
docs.text:
From General_Autonomy_Special_Robots/:

sg docker -c "./docker/run_sim.sh --task Addverb-Teleop-Cam-v0"

Extra args after run_sim.sh are forwarded to addverb_agent.py — e.g. --leader_addr tcp://<hw-ip>:5556 or --repo_root /workspace/datasets/run1 --episode_num 0.

The container host-mounts: - addverb_isaaclab/config/ → :rw (edit YAMLs on the host, relaunch container). - datasets/ → /workspace/datasets:rw (dataset output).

docs.title: Bare-metal (Hardware PC — physical arm)
docs.text:
cd addverb_isaaclab

# Bring up the CAN interface first (if not already)
../addverb_arms/setup_all_cans.sh

# Canonical publisher
python bridge/bridge_sender.py --config config/bridge_config.yaml

# OR the keyboard-enabled variant (R = reset, S = save & home, E = exit)
python bridge/hardware_bridge_sender.py --interface can0 --model ADD

docs.title: Bare-metal (Sim PC — teleop only)
docs.text:
python addverb_isaaclab/sim_agent/addverb_agent.py \
    --task Addverb-Teleop-Cam-v0 \
    --leader_addr tcp://<hardware-pc-ip>:5556

docs.title: Bare-metal (Sim PC — with data collection)
docs.text:
python addverb_isaaclab/sim_agent/addverb_agent.py \
    --task Addverb-Teleop-Cam-v0 \
    --leader_addr tcp://<hardware-pc-ip>:5556 \
    --repo_root /data/addverb_sim_dataset \
    --episode_num 0

docs.title: Keyboard Controls (hardware_bridge_sender.py)
docs.text:
When launched from a terminal with an X display (or a TTY fallback), the hardware bridge watches for two keys:

Key Effect R Call arm.reset_to_home() on the physical arm and publish cmd:reset → sim env calls env.reset(), re-spawning the cube. E Publish cmd:exit → sim agent shuts down cleanly, bridge homes the arm and exits.

Ctrl+C / SIGTERM are also handled as "exit" so you never leave the physical arm under power unexpectedly.

docs.title: Leader Calibration
docs.text:
Run once (or whenever the leader's home drifts). Easiest path — the launcher reads launch_config.yaml + bridge_config.yaml, activates the conda env, and backs up any existing calibration.yaml:

./scripts/launch_calibration.sh
# overrides:
INTERFACE=can1 ./scripts/launch_calibration.sh
MODEL=ADD      ./scripts/launch_calibration.sh

Or call the underlying script directly:

python bridge/hardware_bridge_sender.py \
    --interface can0 --model ADD --calibrate

The flow: 1. You move the leader to the sim home pose (all 6 arm joints centred), press ENTER. 2. The bridge averages 50 samples and computes joint_offset_rad. 3. Move the gripper to its fully-OPEN mechanical pose (the slider-crank zero), press ENTER → physical_open_rad. 4. Values are written to config/calibration.yaml.

KinematicMapper automatically overlays calibration.yaml on top of agent_config.yaml the next time the sim agent starts ([KinematicMapper] overlaid calibration from ... log line). Hardware actuator angle theta_raw maps to the slide position via the slider-crank equation:

theta   = theta_raw - physical_open_rad
x_mm    = 35 * (1.6128 - cos theta - sqrt(1.249 - sin^2 theta))
slide_m = clamp(x_mm / 1000, 0, 0.05)

The same slide_m drives BOTH slide_1_joint and slide_2_joint each step (the env writes the targets independently — no mimic — and the USD authors slide_2's joint frame flipped 180° about X so equal positive joint values produce mirrored world motion = paddles separate).

docs.title: Hardware-Free Testing (Mock Mode)
docs.text:
You can exercise the full simulation + logging pipeline without physical Addverb hardware using the mock publisher.

# Terminal 1 — mock sender (static zeros or slow sine)
python bridge/mock_bridge_sender.py --sine

# Terminal 2 — sim agent
python addverb_isaaclab/sim_agent/addverb_agent.py \
    --task Addverb-Teleop-Cam-v0 \
    --leader_addr tcp://127.0.0.1:5556

docs.title: MDP (sim_agent/mdp/)
docs.text:
The MDP layer is split into observation / reset / termination modules, following the Isaac Lab MDP convention:

  • obs.py — image, image_raw, ee_frame_state, cube_position.
  • resets.py — reset_joints_by_offset, random_asset_pose, reset_cube_on_table, plus the camera / light DR helpers.
  • terms.py — cube_grasped, cube_lifted, cube_lifted_termination (position-based detection — no ContactSensor required).

terms.cube_lifted_termination is not wired into the default env. For teleop you drive episode boundaries with the bridge R key; enabling the termination would cause the sim follower to auto-reset mid-motion. Add it when you want headless / RL-style evaluation:

from isaaclab.managers import TerminationTermCfg as DoneTerm
from .mdp import cube_lifted_termination

@configclass
class AddverbTerminationsCfg:
    cube_lifted = DoneTerm(
        func=cube_lifted_termination,
        params={"table_top_z": 0.4, "lift_height": 0.15, "confirm_steps": 25},
    )

docs.title: Edge-Case Handling (Agent + Bridge)
docs.text:
• Malformed ZMQ frames — the receiver thread validates shape / finiteness and logs rate-limited parse errors rather than dying.
  • Stale leader link — if no frame arrives for > 1 s the agent holds the last command and warns; recovery is logged when frames resume.
  • NaN / non-finite joint values — caught in the bridge and the agent.
  • Control messages — the bridge publishes {"cmd": "reset"} and {"cmd": "exit"} on the same socket; the agent acts on them before applying any pending joint command.
  • Clean shutdown — SIGINT / SIGTERM latch an exit request; the bridge publishes cmd:exit, homes the arm, closes sockets.

docs.title: Collecting Data
docs.text:
End-to-end smoke-tested at 30 Hz; output is fully compatible with addverb_vla_datacollection (same helper functions, same directory tree, same parquet schema). A single recording session of N episodes ends up as a single growing dataset — parquets are appended, info.json totals are kept in sync, and each episode gets its own episode_000/, episode_001/, ... subfolder under every camera.

    Idle --> Recording: launch sim with --repo_root + --episode_num
    Recording --> Recording: teleop → 30 Hz Parquet rows + JPEGs
    Recording --> Recording: R = reset env (cube re-spawn, episode keeps growing)

docs.title: 1. Confirm prerequisites
docs.text:
• Hardware PC: bridge up and publishing, leader calibrated (--calibrate at least once — see Leader Calibration above).
  • Sim PC: the Docker image is built; datasets/ exists at the project root (the run script creates it on first launch).

docs.title: 2. Launch the sim with logging enabled
docs.text:
From General_Autonomy_Special_Robots/:

# Bridge + sim on the same machine (the common case — default works):
sg docker -c "./docker/run_sim.sh \
    --task Addverb-Teleop-Cam-v0 \
    --repo_root /workspace/datasets/session_001 \
    --episode_num 0"

If the bridge runs on a different host, set --leader_addr explicitly. Wrap the URL in quotes or avoid angle-bracket placeholders — an unquoted <hardware-pc-ip> is treated as input redirection by bash:

sg docker -c "./docker/run_sim.sh \
    --task Addverb-Teleop-Cam-v0 \
    --leader_addr 'tcp://10.0.4.117:5556' \
    --repo_root /workspace/datasets/session_001 \
    --episode_num 0"
  • The default --leader_addr (tcp://localhost:5556, set in agent_config.yaml) works because run_sim.sh uses --network=host.
  • --repo_root must be inside /workspace/datasets/ (mounted read-write from General_Autonomy_Special_Robots/datasets/ on the host). The first episode creates the folder; later episodes append.
  • --episode_num starts at 0 and should be incremented by hand for each new recording (the logger does not auto-increment across agent restarts). See "Multi-episode recording" below for the pattern.
  • --task Addverb-Teleop-Cam-v0 (note: -Cam) is required for logging — Addverb-Teleop-v0 omits the cameras.

docs.title: 3. Teleoperate
docs.text:
• Two floating viewport windows (WristCam, TableCam) open next to the main Isaac Sim viewport. Whatever they show is exactly what the dataset is capturing.
  • Drive the Addverb leader to perform the task (pick up the red cube, for example). The agent maps leader joints → sim actions every physics step; the logger drains the most recent frame at 30 Hz and writes it out.
  • Press R on the keyboard that owns the bridge to reset both the physical arm and the sim env between attempts. The current episode's rows stay in memory; they are only flushed to parquet on finalize() (exit or cmd:exit).
  • Console shows a rate-limited log line: [SimDataLogger] frame=... arm=... gripper=....

docs.title: 4. End the episode
docs.text: Press E on the bridge keyboard. The sim receives cmd:exit, calls logger.finalize(), and shuts down. finalize() appends the buffered rows to data/chunk-000/file-000.parquet, updates meta/episodes/.../file-000.parquet, and rewrites meta/info.json with the new total_episodes and total_frames.

docs.title: 5. Multi-episode recording
docs.text:
Increment --episode_num for each recording and keep --repo_root the same:

# episode 0
./docker/run_sim.sh --task Addverb-Teleop-Cam-v0 \
    --repo_root /workspace/datasets/session_001 --episode_num 0

# episode 1 (after exiting the previous session)
./docker/run_sim.sh --task Addverb-Teleop-Cam-v0 \
    --repo_root /workspace/datasets/session_001 --episode_num 1

Each run produces one episode. Stale --episode_num values overwrite images into the same episode_XXX/ folder but add a new row to the episodes parquet — pick numbers you haven't used yet in that session.

docs.title: 6. Inspect the result
docs.text:
On the host (not inside the container):

# Tree view
tree -L 4 datasets/session_001

# Row count + columns
python -c "
import pandas as pd
df = pd.read_parquet('datasets/session_001/data/chunk-000/file-000.parquet')
print('rows:', len(df))
print('columns:', list(df.columns))
print(df.head(3))
"

# Metadata
python -c "import json; print(json.dumps(json.load(open('datasets/session_001/meta/info.json')), indent=2))"

docs.title: Quick recording checklist
docs.text:
• Leader calibrated (--calibrate recent, config/calibration.yaml present)
  • Bridge running on hardware PC (hardware_bridge_sender.py — keyboard focused)
  • Sim container launched with -Cam task, --leader_addr, --repo_root, --episode_num
  • WristCam + TableCam popup windows visible and updating
  • R resets both arms and respawns the cube
  • E flushes the episode and exits cleanly
  • Dataset folder has meta/, data/chunk-000/, and images/observation.images.{ego,external}/episode_XXX/
  • meta/info.json shows robot_type: addverb_sim_6dof, fps: 30.0, correct total_episodes / total_frames

docs.title: Dataset Format
docs.text:
Output is fully compatible with addverb_vla_datacollection — the sim logger uses the exact same metadata_utils_parquet.py helpers. A dataset captured in sim drops straight into the same pipeline as real-hardware data. See the Output layout section in Data Collection (Sim) for the on-disk tree.

Parquet row schema:

Column Shape Description observation.state.joint_positions [7] Sim follower joint positions — [link_1..link_6 (rad), slide_1 (m)]. slide_2 mirrors -slide_1 every step and is intentionally not logged. action.robot [7] Commanded joint targets in the same layout — 6 arm rad + 1 gripper drive in metres state.base [2] Zeros (no mobile base) action.base [2] Zeros (no mobile base) base.timestamp_us int64 Leader hardware timestamp (µs) timestamp float32 Wall-clock time (seconds) frame_number int64 Monotonic frame counter

Default logging rate is 30 Hz, matching addverb_vla_datacollection. Set dof.base_obs_dim: 2 / dof.base_act_dim: 2 in dataset_config.yaml for cross-compatibility with dual-arm mobile data — the logger zero-pads to [9]-element vectors.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Symptom Fix KeyError: 'joints' in receiver Bridge is publishing the old arm_pos / gripper_pos schema — update to the canonical {joints, ee_pose} payload (already done in hardware_bridge_sender.py). assert actual_dof == n_dof fails Set bridge_config.yaml → robot.n_dof to match the arm's reported DOF Sim arm moves opposite to physical Flip joint_sign_flip[i] in agent_config.yaml Sim arm home ≠ physical home Run --calibrate or hand-tune joint_offset_rad[i] Gripper fully closed but sim shows open Re-run ./scripts/launch_calibration.sh (or hand-edit calibration.yaml → gripper.physical_open_rad). The slider-crank curve is symmetric around theta=0 — wrong reference makes both extremes map to the same slide position. High teleop latency See Rendering / Latency Tuning above ZMQ Address already in use Change zmq.port in bridge_config.yaml Isaac Sim joint names mismatch Run env.unwrapped.scene["robot"].data.joint_names and update agent_config pynput fails with DisplayNameError Launch the bridge inside an X session or rely on the stdin fallback (terminal focus required) Viewport windows (WristCam, TableCam) don't open Older Isaac Sim — check the agent log for the "viewport API unavailable" warning; teleop still works Cube falls through the table table.size[2] and cube.init_pos[2] out of sync — cube z is auto-computed from the table top; verify table.center[2] + table.size[2]/2 matches robot.init_pos[2] launch_bridge.sh aborts with CONDA_BUILD: unbound variable conda activate.d hook references unbound vars; the launcher wraps conda activate with set +u / set -u — keep that pair intact when copying the script Sim jaws move together instead of splitting apart The USD authors slide_2_joint's joint frame flipped 180° about X so positive joint values give opposite world motion. If the flip is missing, re-run tools/configure_gripper_joints.py to re-apply it. connect_*_2 bodies are welded rigidly to their slide via PhysicsFixedJoint; connect_*_1 still hinge from link_7 (itself welded to link_6 via PhysicsFixedJoint) but no longer close the loop. Robot loads with no textures (only flat colours) Visual meshes are .STL for fast Assimp import; DAE textures aren't enabled because the source DAE files are too high-poly. See Step 4 for the decimation path.

docs.title: Data Collection — Addverb → Isaac Sim
docs.text:
End-to-end recipe for capturing teleoperation episodes (hardware leader → Isaac Sim follower) into the canonical Parquet + JPEG dataset format used by addverb_vla_datacollection. Everything below assumes the repo root General_Autonomy_Special_Robots/.

Your browser does not support the video tag.

Above: hardware leader back-driven by hand, Isaac Sim follower mirroring in real time while SimDataLogger writes Parquet rows + JPEGs to disk.

docs.title: What gets recorded
docs.text:
Per frame, written at agent_config.yaml → logging.fps (30 Hz by default), in addverb_vla_datacollection-compatible Parquet + JPEG. Logger queue depth (max_queue_depth) and debug-print frequency (debug_freq_hz) live in the same agent_config.yaml → logging block. Dataset SCHEMA (image dims, jpeg quality, dof layout, task prompts) is configured separately under dataset_config.yaml.

Field Type Shape Meaning observation.state.joint_positions float32 (7,) Sim joint state — link_1..link_6, slide_1 (slide_2 mirrors slide_1, intentionally not logged) action.robot float32 (7,) Commanded joint targets (same order) state.base / action.base float32 (2,) Zeros (no mobile base) base.timestamp_us int64 (1,) Leader hardware timestamp (microseconds) observation.images.ego JPEG path Wrist-mounted RGB observation.images.external JPEG path Fixed isometric workspace RGB

Image size and JPEG quality come from dataset_config.yaml → images (default 320×320, q=95).

docs.title: Prerequisites (one-time per machine)
docs.text:
1. Docker image built (sim PC): 
    ./docker/build_sim.sh              # produces addverb-isaaclab:latest
  2. Conda env on host (hardware PC + sim PC alike): 
    conda env create -f environment.yml      # or pip-install addverb_isaaclab/requirements.txt
conda activate unified_addverb_isaaclab
  3. Robot USD bundle is at addverb_isaaclab/assets/usd/addverb_arm/addverb_gripper_urdf_2.usd (already in repo). scripts/launch_sim.sh aborts early if missing.
  4. CAN bus up (real-mode only): 
    ./addverb_arms/setup_all_cans.sh

docs.title: Prerequisites (one-time per physical setup)
docs.text:
%%{init: {'theme':'base','themeVariables':{'background':'transparent','primaryColor':'#f5f5f5','primaryBorderColor':'#383838','primaryTextColor':'#1f1f1f','lineColor':'#383838','textColor':'#1f1f1f','mainBkg':'#f5f5f5','nodeBkg':'#f5f5f5','nodeBorder':'#383838','clusterBkg':'#fafafa','clusterBorder':'#6b7280','titleColor':'#1f1f1f','edgeLabelBackground':'#ffffff','actorBkg':'#f5f5f5','actorBorder':'#383838','actorTextColor':'#1f1f1f','actorLineColor':'#6b7280','signalColor':'#1f1f1f','signalTextColor':'#1f1f1f','labelBoxBkgColor':'#f5f5f5','labelBoxBorderColor':'#383838','labelTextColor':'#1f1f1f','noteBorderColor':'#B45309','noteBkgColor':'#FEF3C7','noteTextColor':'#78350F'}}}%%
flowchart TB
    A([./scripts/launch_calibration.sh]) --> B[Move arm to sim home pose<br/>all 6 joints centred]
    B --> C[ENTER → average 50 samples<br/>writes joint_offset_rad]
    C --> D{{For each joint i = 1..6}}
    D --> E[Move joint to MAX positive<br/>ENTER]
    E --> F[Move joint to MAX negative<br/>ENTER]
    F --> D
    D -- all 6 done --> G[Writes joint_min_rad / joint_max_rad<br/>per joint, overrides URDF defaults]
    G --> H[Move gripper to fully-OPEN pose<br/>ENTER → physical_open_rad]
    H --> I[Type gripper_offset_m — piston gap at full close<br/>ENTER]
    I --> J[(addverb_isaaclab/config/calibration.yaml<br/>previous file backed up)]
    J --> K([Bridge enters publish loop])

    classDef step fill:#FEF3C7,stroke:#B45309,color:#78350F;
    classDef data fill:#DBEAFE,stroke:#1D4ED8,color:#1E3A8A;
    class B,C,E,F,H,I step
    class G,J data

Calibration aligns hardware joint encoders + the gripper-actuator zero point with the sim. Run once after mounting the arm, and again any time the leader is dismantled or homing drifts.

./scripts/launch_calibration.sh
# overrides:
INTERFACE=can1 ./scripts/launch_calibration.sh
MODEL=ADD      ./scripts/launch_calibration.sh

Interactive prompts:

  1. Move leader arm to the sim home pose (all 6 arm joints centred), press ENTER → averages 50 samples, writes joint_offset_rad.
  2. For each of the 6 arm joints (link_1..link_6), move it to its MAX positive extreme, press ENTER; then to its MAX negative extreme, press ENTER. 12 captures total; the script writes the resulting sim-frame range to joint_min_rad and joint_max_rad per joint. These FULLY OVERRIDE the URDF-mirroring defaults in agent_config.yaml — at sim-agent startup the mapper uses calibrated limits, never the USD-baked ones.
  3. Move the gripper to the fully-OPEN mechanical pose (the slider-crank zero), press ENTER → writes physical_open_rad.
  4. Type the gripper offset (in metres) — distance between slide pistons when the gripper is fully closed, measured with a calliper. Blank to skip. Writes gripper_offset_m.

Output → addverb_isaaclab/config/calibration.yaml (the previous file is backed up to calibration.yaml.bak.<ts>). The launcher then drops into the normal publishing loop, so you can Ctrl+C and start a session, or just let it run and launch the sim in another terminal.

KinematicMapper overlays calibration.yaml on top of agent_config.yaml at sim start. Watch the log for:

[KinematicMapper] overlaid calibration from .../config/calibration.yaml

docs.title: 1. Edit launch_config.yaml (one-line per session)
docs.text:
mode: real           # 'mock' for hardware-free dry run, 'real' for the arm
sim:
  task:        Addverb-Teleop-Cam-v0
  leader_addr: tcp://localhost:5556          # cross-machine: tcp://<hw-ip>:5556
  repo_root:   /workspace/datasets/session_001    # path INSIDE the container
  episode_num: 0
bridge:
  conda_env:   unified_addverb_isaaclab
  config_path: addverb_isaaclab/config/bridge_config.yaml
  mock_sine:   true                          # mock-mode: send slow sine

repo_root is interpreted inside the container — /workspace/datasets/ maps to <repo>/datasets/ on the host (mounted by docker/run_sim.sh).

docs.title: 2. Start the bridge
docs.text:
./scripts/launch_bridge.sh                   # mode from launch_config.yaml
# Or force the mode:
MODE=real ./scripts/launch_bridge.sh
MODE=mock ./scripts/launch_bridge.sh

The bridge stays attached to the terminal, prints joint frames at the configured control.loop_hz (200 Hz), and listens for keyboard control: - R — reset both physical arm (reset_to_home()) and the sim env - S — finalize the current dataset episode, home both arms, and start a fresh episode (auto-increments episode_num). Use this between takes — saves to disk before homing so the homing motion itself is never logged into the episode you keep. - E — clean shutdown of bridge + sim

pynput is used in an X session; if no DISPLAY, an stdin-cbreak fallback takes over (terminal must have focus).

docs.title: 3. Start the sim
docs.text:
./scripts/launch_sim.sh                      # uses launch_config.yaml as-is
EPISODE_NUM=3 ./scripts/launch_sim.sh        # bump episode without editing yaml

The container brings up Isaac Sim with two floating viewport windows (WristCam, TableCam) showing the same RGB streams the logger writes. SimDataLogger starts in a background thread — the first prints look like:

[SimDataLogger] config       : .../dataset_config.yaml
[SimDataLogger]   log image  : 320x320  jpeg_q=95
[SimDataLogger]   cameras    : ['ego', 'external']
[SimDataLogger]   joint/act  : 7/7
[SimDataLogger]   rate       : 30.0 Hz
[SimDataLogger] Episode 0 started → /workspace/datasets/session_001

docs.title: 4. Move the leader, capture, end the episode
docs.text:
• Back-drive the physical leader by hand. Sim follows in real time (effective end-to-end latency: ~50 ms, dominated by sensor render time).
  • The cube spawns randomly on the table at every reset. Pick + place it.
  • Press R on the bridge keyboard to reset for the next take.
  • Press E (or Ctrl+C) to exit cleanly. The logger flushes Parquet rows + writes meta/info.json before the env tears down.

docs.title: 5. Multi-episode
docs.text:
Four options, in order of preference:

  • Press S between takes (recommended) — finalizes the current episode to Parquet+JPEG, increments episode_num, starts a fresh episode, and homes both the physical leader and the sim arm. No Isaac Sim restart needed. Pair with R for an in-episode reset that does not save.

  • Restart with bumped episode_num — EPISODE_NUM=N ./scripts/launch_sim.sh. Use when you also want to reload yaml configs or pick up code changes; pays the Isaac Sim startup cost (~30 s).

  • Press R mid-session — resets the env (cube re-spawns); the current episode keeps growing under the same episode_num. Use for long-running rollouts in one episode.

  • Auto-home reset — if the operator returns the arm to home (all six arm joints within 0.03 rad of zero) for more than 50 frames, the agent triggers an env reset by itself. Useful for hands-off chained takes within the same episode.

docs.title: Output layout
docs.text:
repo_root ends up as:

<repo_root>/
├── meta/
│   ├── info.json                                 # codebase + robot + dof + image config
│   ├── tasks.parquet                             # task name + prompt per episode
│   └── episodes/chunk-000/file-000.parquet       # episode index (start row, length, ...)
├── data/chunk-000/file-000.parquet               # one row per logged frame
└── images/observation.images.<cam>/episode_<N>/<timestamp_us>.jpg

info.json is filled in from dataset_config.yaml: 

{
  "codebase_version": "v3.0",
  "robot_type":      "addverb_sim_6dof",
  "fps":             30.0,
  "joint_dim":       7,
  "action_dim":      7,
  "image_keys":      ["observation.images.ego", "observation.images.external"]
}

tasks.parquet rows come from dataset_config.yaml → task_ranges. The default range covers all episode numbers [0, 9999] with prompt "Pick up the object and place it in the designated location.".

docs.title: Configuration reference
docs.text:
File What lives here launch_config.yaml Single source of truth for launch_*.sh: mode, task, leader_addr, repo_root, episode addverb_isaaclab/config/bridge_config.yaml Physical arm: CAN interface, leader PD gains, ZMQ port, control-loop Hz addverb_isaaclab/config/agent_config.yaml Sim PC: ZMQ subscriber, KinematicMapper (sign flip / offset / gripper), logging addverb_isaaclab/config/isaac_scene_config.yaml Scene physics, robot mount pose, USD path, cameras, actuator gains, table, cube addverb_isaaclab/config/dataset_config.yaml Parquet schema (DOF dims), JPEG image size + quality, logging rate, task prompts addverb_isaaclab/config/calibration.yaml Auto-generated by --calibrate; overlays joint offsets + physical_open_rad on top of agent_config.yaml

Important fields by file:

# agent_config.yaml — KinematicMapper
kinematics:
  isaac_joint_names: [link_1_joint, ..., link_6_joint, slide_1_joint]   # 7 entries
  joint_sign_flip:   [1, -1, 1, 1, -1, 1, 1]                            # per-joint sign
  joint_offset_rad:  [0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0]                              # overlaid by calibration
  joint_min_rad:     [-1.57, ..., -1.57,  0.00]                         # arm rad / slide m
  joint_max_rad:     [ 1.57, ...,  1.57,  0.05]
  gripper:
    physical_open_rad: 0.0193     # actuator angle at fully-OPEN pose (theta=0 reference)
    gripper_offset_m:      0.0        # measured slide-piston gap when fully closed (overlaid by calibration.yaml)

# agent_config.yaml — SimDataLogger operational knobs
logging:
  enabled:         true                          # set true to actually write Parquet/JPEG
  dataset_root:    /workspace/datasets/session_001
  fps:             30.0                          # logger sample rate (Hz)
  debug_freq_hz:   1.0                           # console debug-print rate
  max_queue_depth: 500                           # internal frame queue

# dataset_config.yaml — Dataset schema (single source of truth for prompts)
dataset:
  codebase_version: "v3.0"
  robot_type:       "addverb_sim_6dof"
dof:
  joint_dim:        7
  action_dim:       7
  base_obs_dim:     0
  base_act_dim:     0
images:
  log_width:    320
  log_height:   320
  jpeg_quality: 95
  cameras:      [ego, external]
task_ranges:
  - [0, 9999, "Manipulation",
     "Pick up the object and place it in the designated location."]

docs.title: Gripper kinematics (slider-crank)
docs.text:
Hardware actuator angle θ_raw → simulated slide position slide_m:

theta    = θ_raw  -  physical_open_rad           # calibration zero
x_mm     = 35 · (1.6128 - cos θ - sqrt(1.249 - sin² θ))
x_clamp  = clamp(x_mm / 1000, 0, 0.05)
slide_m  = clamp((0.05 - x_clamp) + gripper_offset_m, 0, 0.05)

The 0.05 - inverts the raw curve so the physical open pose maps to slide=0.05 (jaws OPEN at max travel) and the folded pose maps to slide=0 (jaws CLOSED). The trailing + gripper_offset_m bumps the fully-closed slider value up by the measured piston gap (so simulated jaws sit gripper_offset_m apart at full close instead of fused at zero); the outer clamp keeps the slide inside the URDF [0, 0.05] range. slide_m is written to both slide_1_joint and slide_2_joint each step. The USD authors slide_2's joint frame flipped 180° about X so equal positive joint values produce mirrored world motion (paddles separate). The ImplicitActuator PD gains (isaac_scene_config.yaml → actuators.gripper: stiffness 50000 N/m, damping 2000 N·s/m, effort 500 N) hold both stiffly.

connect_1_2 and connect_2_2 are rigidly welded (PhysicsFixedJoint) to their respective slides — no closed-loop linkage to drive.

docs.title: Mock mode (no hardware)
docs.text:
mode: mock                              # in launch_config.yaml
bridge.mock_sine: true                  # slow sine across all 7 joints

./scripts/launch_bridge.sh              # publishes synthetic joint frames
./scripts/launch_sim.sh                 # sim follows them

Useful for end-to-end logger / pipeline checks without the leader arm.

docs.title: Cross-machine (bridge on a different PC)
docs.text:
scripts/launch_sim.sh runs the container with --network=host so tcp://localhost:5556 always reaches the host's bridge. To put the bridge on a different machine, edit launch_config.yaml:

sim:
  leader_addr: tcp://10.0.4.117:5556    # bridge PC's LAN IP

The bridge config (bridge_config.yaml → zmq.host: 0.0.0.0) already listens on every interface, so no change needed on the bridge side.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Symptom Fix Robot USD not found in launcher Path mismatch — confirm assets/usd/addverb_arm/addverb_gripper_urdf_2.usd exists and isaac_scene_config.yaml → robot.usd_path matches [ZMQLeaderReceiver] leader link stale (Xs); holding pose Bridge died or stopped publishing — restart launch_bridge.sh; sim freezes the last command (no garbage written to dataset) Address already in use on bridge start Another bridge is bound to port 5556 — kill it or change bridge_config.yaml → zmq.port Sim joint names mismatch in agent log Update agent_config.yaml → kinematics.isaac_joint_names to match env.unwrapped.scene["robot"].data.joint_names Calibration overlay isn't applied Look for [KinematicMapper] overlaid calibration from ... in agent log; if missing, check calibration.yaml exists next to agent_config.yaml Sim slides don't open with hardware actuator (a) physical_open_rad wrong — re-calibrate; (b) hardware actuator angle isn't moving past the dead-zone — slider-crank gives x_m=0 for the first ~0.6 rad of motion (clamped negative); verify with KinematicMapper.describe() printout in the agent log Sim jaws move together instead of splitting apart USD slide_2_joint axis-flip lost — re-run tools/configure_gripper_joints.py to re-apply the 180° local-rotation flip pynput fails with DisplayNameError (real bridge, no DISPLAY) Launch the bridge inside an X session, or run with stdin focus and use the cbreak fallback High teleop latency Drop rendering.camera_update_period_s to ~30 ms or lower camera resolution in isaac_scene_config.yaml Cube falls through table table.center[2] + table.size[2]/2 must equal robot.init_pos[2]; cube's z is auto-computed from the table top — re-check both in isaac_scene_config.yaml Pressed S but no new episode started Check the agent log for saved episode N + started episode N+1. If only the saved line appeared, the next SimDataLogger constructor failed — usually a config typo in dataset_config.yaml. Logging stops; restart the agent to recover. Empty / corrupt Parquet at episode end Confirm the agent printed [SimDataLogger] Episode N started; if not, agent_config.yaml → logging.enabled is false or --repo_root isn't passed

docs.title: Addverb Hybrid Isaac Lab Simulator
docs.text: This directory contains the necessary components to run an Isaac Lab (Isaac Sim 5.1) simulation of the Addverb Arm inside a Docker container, seamlessly controlled by hardware or mock data running on the host via ZMQ.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
To avoid dependency conflicts between ROS, the lerobot Python framework, and Isaac Sim's internal frozen Python environment, we use a hybrid architecture:

%%{init: {'theme':'base','themeVariables':{'background':'transparent','primaryColor':'#f5f5f5','primaryBorderColor':'#383838','primaryTextColor':'#1f1f1f','lineColor':'#383838','textColor':'#1f1f1f','mainBkg':'#f5f5f5','nodeBkg':'#f5f5f5','nodeBorder':'#383838','clusterBkg':'#fafafa','clusterBorder':'#6b7280','titleColor':'#1f1f1f','edgeLabelBackground':'#ffffff','actorBkg':'#f5f5f5','actorBorder':'#383838','actorTextColor':'#1f1f1f','actorLineColor':'#6b7280','signalColor':'#1f1f1f','signalTextColor':'#1f1f1f','labelBoxBkgColor':'#f5f5f5','labelBoxBorderColor':'#383838','labelTextColor':'#1f1f1f','noteBorderColor':'#B45309','noteBkgColor':'#FEF3C7','noteTextColor':'#78350F'}}}%%
flowchart LR
    subgraph HOST["HOST — conda: unified_addverb_isaaclab"]
        direction TB
        HW["Real arm via pybind C++ SDK<br/><i>or</i> mock sine-wave generator"]
        BR["bridge_sender / mock_bridge_sender<br/>ZMQ PUB · tcp://0.0.0.0:5556"]
        HW --> BR
    end

    subgraph CON["CONTAINER — addverb-isaaclab:latest<br/>nvcr.io/nvidia/isaac-lab:2.3.2"]
        direction TB
        AG["addverb_agent.py<br/>(ZMQ SUB)"]
        SIM["Isaac Lab / Isaac Sim 5.1<br/>(no lerobot deps)"]
        DL["Data Logger<br/>metadata_utils_parquet.py<br/>→ Parquet + JPEG"]
        AG --> SIM --> DL
    end

    BR == "ZMQ JSON @ 200 Hz" ==> AG

    style HOST fill:#EFF6FF,stroke:#1D4ED8,color:#1E3A8A
    style CON  fill:#ECFDF5,stroke:#047857,color:#064E3B
  1. Isaac Lab Simulation (Container): Runs natively inside the NVIDIA base image (nvcr.io/nvidia/isaac-lab:2.3.2). Contains zero dependencies on lerobot_framework.
  2. Hardware / Mock Bridge (Host): Reads the real arm state via the Pybind C++ SDK (or generates mock sine-wave data) and publishes it via a highly efficient ZeroMQ (tcp://0.0.0.0:5556) PUB/SUB topic.
  3. Data Logger (Container): Mirrored from the physical hardware (metadata_utils_parquet.py). Automatically saves simulation observations, states, and camera images into the exact Parquet directory structure used by the hardware setup.

docs.title: 1. Zero-Configuration Build
docs.text:
Build the addverb-isaaclab:latest container:

cd /home/vla-training/isaac_vla/General_Autonomy_Special_Robots
./docker/build_sim.sh

The robot USD lives in the repo at addverb_isaaclab/assets/usd/addverb_arm/ and is mounted into the container at runtime. No URDF→USD conversion happens at launch.

docs.title: 2. Robot Asset
docs.text:
The pre-converted robot USD lives at: 

addverb_isaaclab/assets/usd/addverb_arm/addverb_gripper_urdf_2.usd
with sub-USDs in configuration/ (base + physics + sensor). run_sim.sh errors out early if this file is missing. Replace the bundle in place if you need to update the robot.

docs.title: 3. Running the Pipeline — the easy way (single config)
docs.text:
%%{init: {'theme':'base','themeVariables':{'background':'transparent','primaryColor':'#f5f5f5','primaryBorderColor':'#383838','primaryTextColor':'#1f1f1f','lineColor':'#383838','textColor':'#1f1f1f','mainBkg':'#f5f5f5','nodeBkg':'#f5f5f5','nodeBorder':'#383838','clusterBkg':'#fafafa','clusterBorder':'#6b7280','titleColor':'#1f1f1f','edgeLabelBackground':'#ffffff','actorBkg':'#f5f5f5','actorBorder':'#383838','actorTextColor':'#1f1f1f','actorLineColor':'#6b7280','signalColor':'#1f1f1f','signalTextColor':'#1f1f1f','labelBoxBkgColor':'#f5f5f5','labelBoxBorderColor':'#383838','labelTextColor':'#1f1f1f','noteBorderColor':'#B45309','noteBkgColor':'#FEF3C7','noteTextColor':'#78350F'}}}%%
flowchart LR
    YAML[("launch_config.yaml<br/>mode · task · repo_root · episode_num")]
    LB["./scripts/launch_bridge.sh<br/>(Terminal 1, host)"]
    LS["./scripts/launch_sim.sh<br/>(Terminal 2, container)"]
    BR["mock_bridge_sender.py<br/><i>or</i> hardware_bridge_sender.py"]
    SIM["addverb_agent.py inside<br/>addverb-isaaclab:latest"]
    DS[("datasets/<br/>parquet + JPEG")]

    YAML --> LB --> BR
    YAML --> LS --> SIM
    BR == "ZMQ tcp://localhost:5556" ==> SIM
    SIM --> DS

A pair of launcher scripts reads launch_config.yaml at the repo root and drives everything from one file:

# Terminal 1 — mock (or real) bridge on the HOST
./scripts/launch_bridge.sh

# Terminal 2 — containerised sim
./scripts/launch_sim.sh

Switch mode: mock ↔ mode: real in launch_config.yaml to toggle between mock_bridge_sender.py and the real hardware bridge. Per-run overrides: EPISODE_NUM=3 ./scripts/launch_sim.sh, MODE=real ./scripts/launch_bridge.sh.

docs.title: Manual pipeline (without launchers)
docs.text:
Mock sine-wave test (host): 

conda activate unified_addverb_isaaclab
python addverb_isaaclab/bridge/mock_bridge_sender.py --sine

Real arm (host, CAN bus): 

conda activate unified_addverb_isaaclab
python addverb_isaaclab/bridge/hardware_bridge_sender.py --interface can0 --model ADD

Sim (container, separate terminal): 

./docker/run_sim.sh --task Addverb-Teleop-Cam-v0

The graphical viewport opens via X11 forwarding. The arm tracks whatever the bridge publishes.

docs.title: 4. Collecting Data
docs.text:
Pass --repo_root + --episode_num to run_sim.sh and the SimDataLogger writes the same parquet + JPEG layout the real-robot pipeline uses:

./docker/run_sim.sh --task Addverb-Teleop-Cam-v0 \
    --repo_root /workspace/datasets/session_001 --episode_num 0

You'll get a datasets/session_001/ tree with meta/info.json, meta/tasks.parquet, data/chunk-000/file-000.parquet, and per-camera images/observation.images.<cam>/episode_NNN/*.jpg.

docs.title: QDD Arms + Isaac Lab — Hybrid Development Environment
docs.text: Hardware on host. Simulation in Docker. Connected by ZMQ.

docs.title: Documentation map
docs.text:
This README is the Overview page of the docs site. The full set of operator guides is published at docs.local:

Page What it covers Overview This file — architecture, prerequisites, quick start, validation, project layout, configuration reference, troubleshooting. Architecture & Setup Detailed addverb_isaaclab/ package layout, scene/USD/camera config, full pipeline run-through. Isaac Lab Notes Hybrid host + container architecture, GPU/X11 passthrough, Docker build internals. Data Collection (Sim) Hardware leader → Isaac Sim follower; calibration, 3-terminal flow, Parquet+JPEG output, multi-episode. Real-Hardware Data Collection Same dataset format captured on the physical arm with USB cameras (no sim). Sim-to-Real Training Domain randomisation → GR00T N1.5 fine-tune → sim eval → real eval → co-training.

If you are new, read this page, then jump to whichever page matches your task — calibration & teleop go to Data Collection (Sim), physical-arm captures go to Real-Hardware Data Collection, and training/eval flows live in Sim-to-Real Training.

docs.title: Full sim-to-real pipeline at a glance
docs.text:
The repository covers the complete sim-to-real loop for the 7-DoF QDD arm (6 active revolute joints + 1 welded wrist flange in sim + 1 prismatic gripper). Five stages, each documented in detail on its own page:

%%{init: {'theme':'base','themeVariables':{'background':'transparent','primaryColor':'#f5f5f5','primaryBorderColor':'#383838','primaryTextColor':'#1f1f1f','lineColor':'#383838','textColor':'#1f1f1f','mainBkg':'#f5f5f5','nodeBkg':'#f5f5f5','nodeBorder':'#383838','clusterBkg':'#fafafa','clusterBorder':'#6b7280','titleColor':'#1f1f1f','edgeLabelBackground':'#ffffff','actorBkg':'#f5f5f5','actorBorder':'#383838','actorTextColor':'#1f1f1f','actorLineColor':'#6b7280','signalColor':'#1f1f1f','signalTextColor':'#1f1f1f','labelBoxBkgColor':'#f5f5f5','labelBoxBorderColor':'#383838','labelTextColor':'#1f1f1f','noteBorderColor':'#B45309','noteBkgColor':'#FEF3C7','noteTextColor':'#78350F'}}}%%
flowchart LR
    S1["<b>1. CAPTURE</b><br/>Teleop with DR<br/>(or real arm)<br/>→ Parquet + JPEG"]
    S2["<b>2. TRAIN</b><br/>GR00T N1.6 fine-tune<br/>50–200 demos<br/>10 000 steps · 24 GB+ GPU"]
    S3["<b>3. EVAL (sim)</b><br/>Same env + DR<br/>≥ 50–70 % target<br/>over ≥ 30 rollouts"]
    S4["<b>4. EVAL (real)</b><br/>Same policy server<br/>+ physical arm client"]
    S5["<b>5. CO-TRAIN</b><br/>Mix few real +<br/>many sim demos"]

    S1 --> S2 --> S3 --> S4
    S4 -- if sim-to-real gap is large --> S5
    S5 -- back to (2) --> S2

    classDef capture fill:#FEF3C7,stroke:#B45309,color:#78350F;
    classDef train   fill:#DBEAFE,stroke:#1D4ED8,color:#1E3A8A;
    classDef eval    fill:#DCFCE7,stroke:#15803D,color:#14532D;
    classDef cotrain fill:#FCE7F3,stroke:#BE185D,color:#831843;
    class S1 capture;
    class S2 train;
    class S3,S4 eval;
    class S5 cotrain;
Stage Doc page What it produces 1a. Sim capture (with DR) Data Collection (Sim) addverb_sim_dataset/ — Parquet + JPEG, 30 Hz, 7-DoF, optional randomised lighting/camera focal/camera pose 1b. Real-hardware capture Real-Hardware Data Collection addverb_single_arm_dataset/ — same schema, two USB cameras (top + wrist) 2. GR00T N1.6 fine-tune Sim-to-Real Training § Stage 2 A LoRA / fine-tuned checkpoint (10 000 steps, batch 32, lr 1e-4) 3. Sim evaluation Sim-to-Real Training § Stage 3 Success rate over ≥ 30 rollouts in Addverb-Teleop-Cam-v0-Eval 4. Real-hardware evaluation Sim-to-Real Training § Stage 4 Real-arm rollout success rate, safety-gated 5. Strategy-2 co-training Sim-to-Real Training § Stage 5 Mixed sim+real dataset; loops back to Stage 2

Domain-randomisation ranges (camera ego focal length, camera external pose, light exposure) live in addverb_isaaclab/config/isaac_scene_config.yaml → domain_randomization. Each block has its own enabled: true|false flag (defaults are false), and the helper functions in addverb_isaaclab/sim_agent/mdp/resets.py are wired into reset events by addverb_isaaclab/sim_agent/addverb_env_cfg.py only for blocks that are enabled. Toggle a single knob without rebuilding the env. Cube spawn position is always randomised at every reset (driven by isaac_scene_config.yaml → cube.spawn_range).

The dataset format is identical between sim and real captures, which is what makes Stage 5 co-training straightforward — point the trainer at a directory containing both Parquet roots and let the sampler do the mixing.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text: Requirement Version OS Ubuntu ≥ 22.04 GPU NVIDIA RTX (Ada / Blackwell recommended) NVIDIA Driver ≥ 525 (your system: 580.126.09) Docker ≥ 24.0 NVIDIA Container Toolkit latest Conda (Miniconda / Mambaforge) any

docs.title: Install NVIDIA Container Toolkit (if not installed)
docs.text:
curl -fsSL https://nvidia.github.io/libnvidia-container/gpgkey | \
    sudo gpg --dearmor -o /usr/share/keyrings/nvidia-container-toolkit-keyring.gpg
curl -s -L https://nvidia.github.io/libnvidia-container/stable/deb/nvidia-container-toolkit.list | \
    sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/nvidia-container-toolkit.list
sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y nvidia-container-toolkit
sudo nvidia-ctk runtime configure --runtime=docker
sudo systemctl restart docker

docs.title: Step 1 — Set up the HOST Conda environment
docs.text:
cd /home/vla-training/isaac_vla/General_Autonomy_Special_Robots

# Create the conda environment (host-side only)
conda env create -f unified_env.yml
conda activate unified_addverb_isaaclab

docs.title: Step 2 — Build the QDD Arms C++ pybind module
docs.text:
conda activate unified_addverb_isaaclab

cd addverb_arms/python_bindings/pybind
mkdir -p build && cd build
cmake ..
make -j$(nproc)
# Output: ../addverb_arms_interface.cpython-311-x86_64-linux-gnu.so

Verify the build: 

cd ../../..  # back to python_bindings/
conda run -n unified_addverb_isaaclab python test_import.py
Expected output: 6 passed, 0 failed

docs.title: Step 3 — Build the Docker image
docs.text:
cd /home/vla-training/isaac_vla/General_Autonomy_Special_Robots

chmod +x docker/build_sim.sh docker/run_sim.sh
./docker/build_sim.sh

First build pulls ~20 GB from NVIDIA's registry. Subsequent builds are cached.

docs.title: Quickest path — single-config launchers
docs.text:
Edit launch_config.yaml at the repo root once (mode: mock|real, image, task, repo_root, etc.), then:

# Terminal A (host) — bridge
./scripts/launch_bridge.sh

# Terminal B — sim
./scripts/launch_sim.sh

Per-run overrides: EPISODE_NUM=3 ./scripts/launch_sim.sh, MODE=real ./scripts/launch_bridge.sh. See scripts/launch_*.sh for the full list of env-var knobs. The sections below describe the manual pipeline if you want to invoke the bridge and container directly.

docs.title: Calibration
docs.text:
./scripts/launch_calibration.sh                  # all defaults from launch_config.yaml + bridge_config.yaml
INTERFACE=can1 ./scripts/launch_calibration.sh   # override CAN interface
MODEL=ADD      ./scripts/launch_calibration.sh

The launcher runs the interactive arm + gripper calibration (joint home, gripper open, gripper closed, optional jaw separation in metres) and writes addverb_isaaclab/config/calibration.yaml. After the prompts the bridge falls into its publishing loop, so you can leave it running and start the sim straight away — press E or Ctrl+C if calibration was all you wanted.

docs.title: Terminal A — Host: Start the bridge sender
Without hardware (mock sine wave for testing):
docs.text:
conda activate unified_addverb_isaaclab
cd /home/vla-training/isaac_vla/General_Autonomy_Special_Robots

python addverb_isaaclab/bridge/mock_bridge_sender.py --sine

docs.title: With real hardware (CAN bus):
docs.text:
conda activate unified_addverb_isaaclab
cd /home/vla-training/isaac_vla/General_Autonomy_Special_Robots

# Ensure CAN interface is up first:
# sudo ip link set can0 up type can bitrate 1000000
python addverb_isaaclab/bridge/bridge_sender.py \
    --config addverb_isaaclab/config/bridge_config.yaml

docs.title: Terminal B — Container: Launch Isaac Sim
docs.text:
The robot is loaded from a pre-converted USD bundle committed under addverb_isaaclab/assets/usd/addverb_arm/addverb_gripper_urdf_2.usd (mounted into the container read-write). No URDF→USD conversion happens at launch. For more detail on the Isaac Lab side, see Isaac Lab Notes.

cd /home/vla-training/isaac_vla/General_Autonomy_Special_Robots

# Basic teleop — no cameras, no recording
./docker/run_sim.sh --task Addverb-Teleop-v0

# With cameras
./docker/run_sim.sh --task Addverb-Teleop-Cam-v0

# With exactly matched hardware-style Data Recording (Parquet + JPEGs)
./docker/run_sim.sh --task Addverb-Teleop-Cam-v0 --repo_root /workspace/datasets/session_001 --episode_num 0

docs.title: Validation Test (No Hardware Required)
docs.text: Run this to confirm host ↔ container communication works end-to-end:

docs.title: Terminal A — start mock bridge
docs.text:
conda activate unified_addverb_isaaclab
cd /home/vla-training/isaac_vla/General_Autonomy_Special_Robots
python addverb_isaaclab/bridge/mock_bridge_sender.py --sine

Expected output: 

[mock_bridge_sender] Publishing on tcp://0.0.0.0:5556 at 200 Hz
[mock_bridge_sender] Mode: Sine wave mock motion
[      0] arm: [0. 0. 0. 0. 0. 0.]  gripper: 0.0000 rad
[   1000] arm: [0.048 0.048 ...]     gripper: 0.0480 rad

docs.title: Terminal B — launch sim container
docs.text:
./docker/run_sim.sh --task Addverb-Teleop-v0

Expected console output in the container: 

[addverb_agent] Task:        Addverb-Teleop-v0
[addverb_agent] Leader addr: tcp://localhost:5556
[ZMQLeaderReceiver] Subscribed to tcp://localhost:5556
[addverb_agent] Running. Close the Isaac Sim window or Ctrl+C to stop.

The Isaac Sim viewport will open and the robot arm will mirror the sine wave motion from the mock sender.

docs.title: Project Layout
docs.text:
General_Autonomy_Special_Robots/
├── unified_env.yml                  # HOST Conda environment (no Isaac Sim binaries)
├── docker/
│   ├── Dockerfile                   # Isaac Lab sim container definition
│   ├── build_sim.sh                 # Build helper
│   └── run_sim.sh                   # Run helper (GPU + X11 + --network=host)
├── addverb_arms/                    # C++ arm driver (host only, NOT in container)
│   ├── CMakeLists.txt
│   ├── src/                         # C++ sources (control, kd, lower_level, etc.)
│   └── python_bindings/
│       ├── pybind/
│       │   ├── CMakeLists.txt       # Builds addverb_arms_interface.cpython-311.so
│       │   └── addverb_arms_pybind.cpp
│       └── test_import.py           # Sanity test for the pybind module
├── addverb_isaaclab/                # Pure-Python sim-side package (also copied into container)
│   ├── bridge/
│   │   ├── bridge_sender.py         # HOST: reads arm → ZMQ PUB
│   │   └── mock_bridge_sender.py    # HOST: mock arm → ZMQ PUB (no hardware)
│   ├── sim_agent/
│   │   ├── addverb_agent.py         # CONTAINER: ZMQ SUB → Isaac Lab
│   │   └── addverb_env_cfg.py       # Isaac Lab env configs
│   ├── utils/
│   │   ├── kinematic_mapper.py      # Joint space remapping (Addverb -> Isaac Sim)
│   │   └── config_loader.py
│   ├── dataset/                     # Parquet/JPEG data logger (sim-side)
│   └── config/
│       ├── agent_config.yaml        # ZMQ addr, task, logging, kinematics
│       ├── bridge_config.yaml       # CAN interface, ZMQ port, loop Hz
│       └── isaac_scene_config.yaml  # Robot USD path, cameras, physics
└── lerobot_framework/               # LeRobot framework + reference task scaffolding (also in container)
    └── source/sim_to_real_so101/

docs.title: addverb_isaaclab/config/bridge_config.yaml
docs.text:
Controls the host-side bridge sender.

Key Default Description robot.can_interface can0 CAN bus device name robot.n_dof 7 Arm DOF (6 arm + 1 gripper) zmq.host 0.0.0.0 Bind address (all interfaces) zmq.port 5556 ZMQ PUB port control.loop_hz 200 Publishing rate

docs.title: addverb_isaaclab/config/agent_config.yaml
docs.text:
Controls the container-side Isaac Lab agent.

Key Default Description env.task Addverb-Teleop-v0 Gym environment ID env.device cuda:0 Torch compute device zmq.leader_addr tcp://localhost:5556 Bridge sender address logging.enabled false Dataset recording toggle logging.dataset_root /tmp/addverb_sim_dataset Dataset output path

docs.title: Dependency Conflict Notes
docs.text: Conflict Resolution numpy 2.x vs Isaac Sim 1.26.0 Host env uses <2.0; container base pins 1.26.0 torch 2.5.x (cu124) vs Isaac Sim 2.7.0 Separate environments — no conflict opencv-python vs headless envs Use conda opencv package (no Qt) on host; pip headless in container lerobot datasets requirement Installed inside container only; host doesn't need it torchvision==0.20.1 vs lerobot >=0.21.0 Relaxed to >=0.20.1 on host; container uses base image version

docs.title: Isaac Sim window doesn't appear
docs.text:
xhost +local:docker
# Re-run docker/run_sim.sh

docs.title: ZMQ: agent shows joints is None (no data received)
docs.text:
1. Confirm bridge_sender is running on the host
  2. Confirm port 5556 is not blocked: ss -tlnp | grep 5556
  3. With --network=host, localhost in the container == host machine ✓

docs.title: Docker can't access GPU
docs.text:
nvidia-smi  # verify driver works
docker run --rm --gpus all nvidia/cuda:12.6.0-base-ubuntu22.04 nvidia-smi

docs.title: launch_bridge.sh aborts with CONDA_BUILD: unbound variable
docs.text: The conda activate.d hook ros-noetic-catkin_activate.sh references $CONDA_BUILD, $target_platform, $build_platform without defaults, and set -u in the launcher trips on them. The launcher already wraps the conda activate call with set +u / set -u; if you copy this script into a new project make sure that pair stays intact.

docs.title: CAN interface not found
docs.text:
# Bring up CAN interface
sudo ip link set can0 up type can bitrate 1000000
# Or use the provided setup script:
bash addverb_arms/setup_slcan.sh

docs.title: Real-Hardware Data Collection — Single Leader + Single Follower
docs.text:
End-to-end recipe for capturing teleoperation episodes on physical hardware only (no Isaac Sim, no AMR base): one leader arm drives one follower arm, two USB cameras (top + wrist) stream synchronised RGB, and every episode lands in the same LeRobot-flavoured Parquet + JPEG layout used by the dual-arm pipeline. The follower arm has 6 active joints + a parallel-jaw gripper (link_7 is welded as a fixed flange in sim — irrelevant on hardware, where it has never been actuated).

The whole pipeline runs inside a self-contained Docker container — the host only needs Docker and the SocketCAN devices wired up.

Source path: addverb_arms/addverb_vla_datacollection/ Image: addverb-datacollect:latest (built from Dockerfile.datacollect in the same directory)

Your browser does not support the video tag.

docs.title: 1. What gets recorded
docs.text:
Per frame, written at config_single.LOGGING_RATE_HZ (default 30 Hz):

Field Type Shape Meaning index int64 (1,) Global frame index across all episodes episode_index int64 (1,) Episode number this row belongs to frame_index int64 (1,) Index within this episode task_index int64 (1,) Resolved from EPISODE_TASK_RANGES timestamp float32 (1,) Wall-clock seconds (epoch) observation.state.joint_positions float32 (7,) Follower joints j0..j5 + gripper action.robot float32 (7,) Leader joints j0..j5 + gripper (commanded) observation.images.camera1_images image (H,W,3) Top camera (default 320×320) observation.images.camera2_images image (H,W,3) Wrist-mounted camera

The schema is identical to the dual-arm pipeline minus the second arm and the AMR base — driven entirely by config_single.py (NUM_ARMS=1, BASE_OBS_DIM=0, BASE_ACT_DIM=0, JOINT_DIM=7, ACTION_DIM=7).

Local-only mode (no remote streaming):

addverb_vla_datacollection/
├── episodes/                                    ← one folder per saved take
│   ├── episode_000/
│   │   ├── data.csv                             ← raw per-frame log (CSV)
│   │   ├── camera1_images/<timestamp>.jpg       ← top
│   │   ├── camera2_images/<timestamp>.jpg       ← left
│   │   └── camera3_images/<timestamp>.jpg       ← right
│   └── episode_001/...
└── addverb_single_arm_dataset/                  ← LeRobot-style parquet
    ├── meta/
    │   ├── info.json                            ← schema + counts
    │   ├── tasks.parquet
    │   └── episodes/chunk-000/file-000.parquet
    └── data/chunk-000/file-000.parquet          ← all frames, all episodes

Remote-streaming mode (REMOTE_TRANSFER_ENABLED=True): camera frames + rows go straight to remote_transfer_server.py over TCP, and the parquet files are written on the remote host. The local episodes/ dir only holds an empty marker folder per saved take.

docs.title: 3. Files in this pipeline
docs.text:
addverb_vla_datacollection/
├── config.py                ← dual-arm config (unchanged)
├── config_single.py         ← single-arm config (NEW)
├── data-master.py           ← orchestrator (patched, fully back-compat)
├── data-collect.py          ← recorder    (patched, --single-arm/--no-base flags)
├── logger_thread.py         ← logger      (patched, base_client now optional)
├── camera_thread.py         ← unchanged
├── camera_config.json       ← 3 USB cameras
├── metadata_utils_parquet.py← unchanged (parquet writer)
├── remote_transfer_client.py← unchanged (TCP streamer)
├── collect_data_single.bash ← single-arm launcher (NEW)
├── Dockerfile.datacollect   ← container image (NEW)
└── run_datacollect_docker.sh← container launcher (NEW)

The single-arm flow does not duplicate the dual-arm code. It re-uses data-master.py, data-collect.py, logger_thread.py, etc. by:

  1. Adding --single-arm and --no-base flags to data-collect.py / data-master.py so the second arm pair and the AMR base client are simply not constructed.
  2. Making base_client truly optional inside DataLoggingThread — when None, the base-sync gate is skipped and state.base / action.base / base.timestamp_us columns are dropped from every row.
  3. Aliasing import config → config_single for the duration of one run via a mktemp-staged shim prepended to PYTHONPATH inside collect_data_single.bash. All downstream modules (logger_thread, camera_thread, metadata_utils_parquet, …) pick up the single-arm dimensions and the addverb_single_arm_dataset name without code changes.
