docs.title: 4.1 Docker
docs.text:
docker --version       # 24+ recommended
groups | grep docker   # current user must be in the docker group

If you are not in the docker group, the run script automatically retries itself under sg docker -c ….

docs.title: 4.2 SocketCAN
docs.text:
Two CAN interfaces are required:

Role Interface Leader can0 Follower can1

Bring them up with the helper that ships with the repo:

sudo /home/vla-training/isaac_vla/General_Autonomy_Special_Robots/addverb_arms/setup_all_cans.sh

run_datacollect_docker.sh calls this for you on every launch, so you do not have to remember it.

docs.title: 4.3 USB cameras
docs.text:
Plug in three V4L2-compatible cameras and run once:

cd addverb_arms/addverb_vla_datacollection
python3 assign_cameras.py

This writes /dev/v4l/by-path/... paths into camera_config.json for the three logical roles cam1 (top), cam2 (left), cam3 (right). Persistent by-path entries survive reboots and USB port changes.

docs.title: 5. First-time build of the container image
docs.text:
The image bakes in:

  • Ubuntu 22.04 (no ROS)
  • liborocos-kdl-dev, libkdl-parser-dev, liburdf-dev, liburdfdom-dev from the Ubuntu universe repo
  • libeigen3-dev, libboost-all-dev, pybind11-dev, build-essentials
  • Python 3.10 + numpy, scipy, pandas, pyarrow, opencv-python, click, pybind11
  • The full addverb_arms/ source tree, with the C++ pybind module compiled at image-build time into python_bindings/pybind/addverb_arms_interface.cpython-310-x86_64-linux-gnu.so
cd addverb_arms/addverb_vla_datacollection
./run_datacollect_docker.sh                # builds the image if missing
# or, to force a clean rebuild after changing C++ source:
./run_datacollect_docker.sh --rebuild

Build context is addverb_arms/ (the parent dir). The companion addverb_arms/.dockerignore strips host-side build trees so the context stays small.

Verify the image and the .so:

docker images addverb-datacollect:latest

docker run --rm addverb-datacollect:latest \
    bash -lc 'python3 -c "import sys; \
        sys.path.insert(0, \"/workspace/addverb_arms/python_bindings/pybind\"); \
        import addverb_arms_interface as m; \
        print([n for n in dir(m) if not n.startswith(\"_\")])"'
# → ['AddverbArmsJointController', 'ControllerConfig', 'Gain', 'JointState', 'RobotConfig']

docs.title: 6.1 Local-only mode (default)
docs.text:
Everything is saved on the host disk.

cd addverb_arms/addverb_vla_datacollection
./run_datacollect_docker.sh

docs.title: 6.2 Remote-streaming mode
docs.text:
Set REMOTE_TRANSFER_ENABLED = True in config_single.py (or pass --remote-host), then start remote_transfer_server.py on the remote PC, then:

./run_datacollect_docker.sh --remote-host 192.168.0.56 --remote-port 9200

The launcher pre-checks reachability and refuses to start the recorder if the remote server is down.

docs.title: 6.3 The interactive loop
docs.text:
%%{init: {'theme':'base','themeVariables':{'background':'transparent','primaryColor':'#f5f5f5','primaryBorderColor':'#383838','primaryTextColor':'#1f1f1f','lineColor':'#383838','textColor':'#1f1f1f','mainBkg':'#f5f5f5','nodeBkg':'#f5f5f5','nodeBorder':'#383838','clusterBkg':'#fafafa','clusterBorder':'#6b7280','titleColor':'#1f1f1f','edgeLabelBackground':'#ffffff','actorBkg':'#f5f5f5','actorBorder':'#383838','actorTextColor':'#1f1f1f','actorLineColor':'#6b7280','signalColor':'#1f1f1f','signalTextColor':'#1f1f1f','labelBoxBkgColor':'#f5f5f5','labelBoxBorderColor':'#383838','labelTextColor':'#1f1f1f','noteBorderColor':'#B45309','noteBkgColor':'#FEF3C7','noteTextColor':'#78350F'}}}%%
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Ready: container start
    Ready --> Recording: ENTER (start episode)
    Ready --> Ready: r (reset arms only)
    Ready --> [*]: q (quit)
    Recording --> Drained: e or Ctrl+C — drain queue, flush CSV/parquet, home arms
    Drained --> Saved: y (save episode)
    Drained --> Discarded: n (discard)
    Saved --> Decision
    Discarded --> Decision
    Decision --> Ready: y (record another)
    Decision --> [*]: n (done)

After the container starts you'll see, in order:

  1. READY TO RECORD EPISODE 0 → Press ENTER to start recording (r=reset arms, q=quit):
  2. The recorder spawns: arms home, gains are set, cameras warm up for 3 s, then teleoperation begins. Stats line prints once per second: samples, Hz, skipped, pending (camera queue depth), cam_saved, cam_failures.
  3. To finish the episode, press e (preferred) or Ctrl+C. The recorder drains the camera queue, flushes the local CSV / parquet (or remote stream), then homes both arms.
  4. Save this episode? (y=save / n=discard):
  5. y → CSV + JPEG folders are moved to episodes/episode_NNN/ and the row batch is appended to addverb_single_arm_dataset/data/chunk-000/file-000.parquet; meta/info.json and meta/episodes/... get updated.
  6. n → data.csv and the camera folders are deleted; the remote server is told to discard staged data.
  7. Record another episode? (y/n):

To abort partway through the loop without recording, press q at the first prompt. r re-homes the arms without starting an episode.

docs.title: 7. Container privileges (for reference)
docs.text:
run_datacollect_docker.sh launches with everything the recorder needs:

Flag Why --privileged SocketCAN, USB --network=host Reach remote_transfer_server over LAN --ipc=host Shared-memory frame buffers (/dev/shm) --pid=host Cleaner shutdown & camera viewer process tree --cap-add NET_ADMIN/NET_RAW/SYS_RAWIO/SYS_ADMIN Bring up CAN, raw frame access --device=/dev/videoN Each detected USB camera -v /dev/v4l:/dev/v4l:ro Persistent by-path camera names -v /dev/bus/usb:/dev/bus/usb All USB devices (cameras, ttyACM) -v /tmp/.X11-unix + ~/.Xauthority Optional camera_viewer.py windows on host -v <data_collection_dir>:/workspace/.../addverb_vla_datacollection Live edits without rebuild

The pybind .so is baked into the image under /workspace/addverb_arms/python_bindings/pybind/. We deliberately do NOT bind-mount the pybind directory — that would shadow the built artifact.

docs.title: 8. Configuration knobs
docs.text:
All of the following live in config_single.py:

Constant Default Purpose LOGGING_RATE_HZ 30.0 Frames per second logged CONTROL_LOOP_RATE_HZ 200.0 Leader→follower command loop rate CAMERA_WIDTH/HEIGHT 640 × 480 Capture resolution (sensor side) LOG_WIDTH/LOG_HEIGHT 320 × 320 Centre-crop + resize before saving / streaming REMOTE_TRANSFER_ENABLED False Toggle remote streaming REMOTE_HOST/PORT 192.168.0.56:9200 Remote transfer server REMOTE_JPEG_QUALITY 90 JPEG quality used for over-the-wire encoding DATASET_NAME addverb_single_arm_dataset Output parquet root EPISODE_TASK_RANGES [(0, 9999, "Single Arm…", "…")] (start, end, task_name, task_prompt) tuples

Edit EPISODE_TASK_RANGES to attach different task prompts to different episode-number ranges. The parquet task_index column is resolved from this table at write time.

docs.title: Camera config missing
docs.text:
ERROR: camera_config.json not found!

You haven't run assign_cameras.py yet. Do it once on the host (it talks directly to V4L2; no docker needed).

docs.title: CAN interface not visible inside the container
docs.text:
Confirm on the host first:

ip link show | grep '^.*can'

If the interfaces are missing, run setup_all_cans.sh manually and then re-launch the container. --privileged + --network=host is enough for the container to see them — no extra --device flag for can0/1 is required.

docs.title: import addverb_arms_interface fails inside the container
docs.text:
The .so wasn't built. Re-build the image with --rebuild:

./run_datacollect_docker.sh --rebuild

Check the build log around the pybind11_add_module step. Most failures fall into:

  • missing apt deps — they should all be in Dockerfile.datacollect; if you've edited it, double-check liborocos-kdl-dev libkdl-parser-dev liburdf-dev liburdfdom-dev liburdfdom-headers-dev libeigen3-dev libboost-all-dev pybind11-dev.
  • addverb_arms_pybind.cpp won't compile after a C++-side change — fix the source then --rebuild.

docs.title: Remote server unreachable
docs.text:
ERROR: Remote server at <host>:<port> is NOT reachable.

Either start remote_transfer_server.py on the remote PC first, or set REMOTE_TRANSFER_ENABLED = False in config_single.py and re-launch.

docs.title: Frames being skipped due to camera backlog
docs.text:
The stats line shows pending: [N, N, N] climbing past 10. Either:

  • the host CPU can't keep up encoding 3× JPEGs at 30 Hz — drop LOG_WIDTH/HEIGHT or LOGGING_RATE_HZ in config_single.py,
  • or remote streaming is saturated. Check [RemoteClient] queue depth printout — if it stays high, drop REMOTE_JPEG_QUALITY (e.g. 90 → 75).

docs.title: Can I run dual-arm and single-arm from the same checkout?
docs.text:
Yes — none of the patches change default behaviour.

  • Dual-arm: bash collect_data.bash
  • Single-arm: bash collect_data_single.bash (or via the docker launcher)

docs.title: 10. Quick reference
docs.text:
# Build / launch container (does both)
./addverb_arms/addverb_vla_datacollection/run_datacollect_docker.sh

# Force rebuild image after C++ source changes
./addverb_arms/addverb_vla_datacollection/run_datacollect_docker.sh --rebuild

# Stream to a remote PC
./addverb_arms/addverb_vla_datacollection/run_datacollect_docker.sh \
    --remote-host 192.168.0.56 --remote-port 9200

# Run on bare host (no container) — same flags, same output
cd addverb_arms/addverb_vla_datacollection
bash collect_data_single.bash

Episode lifecycle:

ENTER → record → e (or Ctrl+C) → y/n save? → y/n another? → q

docs.title: Sim-to-Real Training Pipeline (DR, GR00T, Co-training)
docs.text:
End-to-end recipe for training a vision-language-action (VLA) policy on top of the Addverb teleoperation stack and deploying it back onto the physical arm. Targets our hardware (7-DoF Addverb arm — 6 active revolute joints plus a welded wrist flange in sim + 1 prismatic gripper) and our dataset (2 USB cameras for single-arm captures, LeRobot Parquet/JPEG via addverb_vla_datacollection).

The recipe has five stages:

%%{init: {'theme':'base','themeVariables':{'background':'transparent','primaryColor':'#f5f5f5','primaryBorderColor':'#383838','primaryTextColor':'#1f1f1f','lineColor':'#383838','textColor':'#1f1f1f','mainBkg':'#f5f5f5','nodeBkg':'#f5f5f5','nodeBorder':'#383838','clusterBkg':'#fafafa','clusterBorder':'#6b7280','titleColor':'#1f1f1f','edgeLabelBackground':'#ffffff','actorBkg':'#f5f5f5','actorBorder':'#383838','actorTextColor':'#1f1f1f','actorLineColor':'#6b7280','signalColor':'#1f1f1f','signalTextColor':'#1f1f1f','labelBoxBkgColor':'#f5f5f5','labelBoxBorderColor':'#383838','labelTextColor':'#1f1f1f','noteBorderColor':'#B45309','noteBkgColor':'#FEF3C7','noteTextColor':'#78350F'}}}%%
flowchart LR
    S1["<b>Stage 1</b><br/>Collect dataset<br/>(DR teleop)"]
    S2["<b>Stage 2</b><br/>Fine-tune GR00T<br/>on the dataset"]
    S3["<b>Stage 3</b><br/>Evaluate in sim<br/>(Isaac Lab env + DR)"]
    S4["<b>Stage 4</b><br/>Evaluate on real<br/>hardware"]
    S5["<b>Stage 5</b><br/>Co-training:<br/>mix few real + many sim demos"]

    S1 --> S2 --> S3 --> S4
    S4 -- gap acceptable --> SHIP([Ship policy])
    S4 -- real-world gap is large --> S5
    S5 -- return to Stage 2 --> S2

    classDef capture fill:#FEF3C7,stroke:#B45309,color:#78350F;
    classDef train   fill:#DBEAFE,stroke:#1D4ED8,color:#1E3A8A;
    classDef eval    fill:#DCFCE7,stroke:#15803D,color:#14532D;
    classDef cotrain fill:#FCE7F3,stroke:#BE185D,color:#831843;
    classDef ship    fill:#E5E7EB,stroke:#6B7280,color:#111827;
    class S1 capture
    class S2 train
    class S3,S4 eval
    class S5 cotrain
    class SHIP ship

The choice between DR-only (Stages 1–4) and co-training (adds Stage 5) is empirical: try DR-only first; only fall through to co-training if real-world success rate is unacceptable.

docs.title: Why DR
docs.text: Real cameras drift, lighting changes, mounts vibrate. A policy trained on a single, perfectly-lit sim scene will overfit to those exact conditions and fail on real hardware. We randomise the sim scene at every reset so the policy can never lean on visual constants — it has to learn the task, not the backdrop.

docs.title: What we already randomise
docs.text:
%%{init: {'theme':'base','themeVariables':{'background':'transparent','primaryColor':'#f5f5f5','primaryBorderColor':'#383838','primaryTextColor':'#1f1f1f','lineColor':'#383838','textColor':'#1f1f1f','mainBkg':'#f5f5f5','nodeBkg':'#f5f5f5','nodeBorder':'#383838','clusterBkg':'#fafafa','clusterBorder':'#6b7280','titleColor':'#1f1f1f','edgeLabelBackground':'#ffffff','actorBkg':'#f5f5f5','actorBorder':'#383838','actorTextColor':'#1f1f1f','actorLineColor':'#6b7280','signalColor':'#1f1f1f','signalTextColor':'#1f1f1f','labelBoxBkgColor':'#f5f5f5','labelBoxBorderColor':'#383838','labelTextColor':'#1f1f1f','noteBorderColor':'#B45309','noteBkgColor':'#FEF3C7','noteTextColor':'#78350F'}}}%%
flowchart LR
    CFG[("isaac_scene_config.yaml<br/>→ domain_randomization:")]
    EVT["addverb_env_cfg.py<br/>EventTermCfg → on reset"]
    HLP["sim_agent/mdp/resets.py<br/>helper functions"]

    CFG --> EVT --> HLP
    HLP --> H1["randomize_camera_focal_length<br/>(camera_ego)"]
    HLP --> H2["randomize_camera_pose<br/>(camera_external)"]
    HLP --> H3["randomize_light_exposure"]
    HLP --> H4["random_asset_pose<br/>(cube spawn)"]

    H1 --> SCENE[(Isaac Lab scene)]
    H2 --> SCENE
    H3 --> SCENE
    H4 --> SCENE

The Addverb env already wires DR into addverb_isaaclab/sim_agent/mdp/resets.py via three event terms in addverb_env_cfg.py:

Helper Purpose YAML knob (under domain_randomization:) randomize_camera_focal_length Perturb FOV of a camera sensor camera_ego_focal_length.{enabled, range_mm} randomize_camera_pose Perturb camera position / orientation camera_external_pose.{enabled, pos_range, rot_range} randomize_light_exposure Vary scene brightness light_exposure.{enabled, range_ev} random_asset_pose Randomise cube/asset pose always-on; driven by cube.spawn_range

Each block has its own enabled: true|false flag (default false); a disabled block adds no EventTermCfg, so the env behaves identically to a non-DR run. Toggle individual knobs without rebuilding anything.

The current YAML in addverb_isaaclab/config/isaac_scene_config.yaml (domain_randomization: block) is:

domain_randomization:
  camera_ego_focal_length:
    enabled: false
    range_mm: [12.0, 15.0]              # mm  (±10 % around 13.5)

  camera_external_pose:
    enabled: false
    pos_range:                          # m, per-axis delta
      x: [-0.02, 0.02]
      y: [-0.02, 0.02]
      z: [-0.01, 0.01]
    rot_range:                          # rad, per-axis delta
      roll:  [-0.05, 0.05]
      pitch: [-0.05, 0.05]
      yaw:   [-0.05, 0.05]

  light_exposure:
    enabled: false
    range_ev: [-3.0, 1.0]

Flip the relevant enabled: true to activate a knob — no other change is required.

docs.title: Recommended ranges
docs.text:
Knob Recommended range Status in our config Light exposure (-4.0, 3.0) We use (-3.0, 1.0) (slightly narrower — widen if real lighting varies more) Light colour temp. (2500.0, 9500.0) K Not yet wired — add a randomize_light_colour_temp helper if HDRI lighting drifts in your real scene HDRI texture procedurally selected from available .exr files Not yet wired — irrelevant if our real workspace is fixed Camera position X / Y (-0.02, 0.02) m Same Camera position Z (-0.01, 0.01) m Same Roll / pitch (-0.05, 0.05) rad Same Yaw (-0.1, 0.1) rad We use (-0.05, 0.05) — widen if external camera mount is hand-tightened in real Object pose randomised per reset Yes — reset_cube_position event term

Physics randomisation categories (object mass, friction, restitution, joint damping, actuator delays, sensor noise) have no fixed numeric ranges; treat that as an open knob if your real arm's torque profile diverges from the URDF.

docs.title: Episode count
docs.text: Plan for 70–150 successful takes per task prompt. Below 50 the policy underfits; beyond 200 the marginal returns flatten.

docs.title: Recording with the data-collection container
docs.text:
For the real-hardware single-arm path the workflow is the run_datacollect_docker.sh loop documented in Real-Hardware Data Collection. For the sim teleop path you publish leader joint frames over ZMQ (addverb_isaaclab/bridge/bridge_sender.py) and the in-container agent (addverb_agent.py) writes the episode.

Step Real-hardware (this repo) Start episode ENTER at the master-script prompt Stop episode press e (or Ctrl+C) Cancel mid-take press q at the next prompt + n to discard Reset arm only r at the master-script prompt

docs.title: Task prompt strings
docs.text:
Pin one English sentence per task and store it as task_prompt. The location depends on which capture path you use:

  • Sim capture (addverb_isaaclab): addverb_isaaclab/config/dataset_config.yaml → task_ranges. Each entry is [start_ep, end_ep, task_name, task_prompt]. SimDataLogger._get_task_prompt(episode_num) resolves the active prompt at log time and stamps it into both each row and meta/tasks.parquet.
  • Real-hardware capture (addverb_vla_datacollection): EPISODE_TASK_RANGES in config_single.py (or config.py for the dual-arm setup).

Update the task_prompt string before recording so the parquet task_index column points at the right description. The default single-arm prompt is:

"Use the leader arm to teleoperate the follower arm and complete the
 manipulation task."

Replace this with the actual task before each recording session — GR00T conditions on it at inference time.

docs.title: Dataset format produced
docs.text:
Per frame, written into the LeRobot v2 layout described in Real-Hardware Data Collection:

  • observation.state.joint_positions — float32 [7] (6 arm joints + gripper)
  • action.robot — float32 [7] (leader joints + gripper)
  • observation.images.camera1_images — JPEG (top)
  • observation.images.camera2_images — JPEG (left)
  • observation.images.camera3_images — JPEG (right)
  • task_index, episode_index, frame_index, timestamp

Our three camera keys (camera1_images, camera2_images, camera3_images) need a rename map at training time to whatever canonical names the trainer expects (e.g. top / left / right) — see Stage 2.

Camera framing. Default is 320×320 (square). If you change LOG_WIDTH/LOG_HEIGHT in config_single.py, keep the aspect ratio sensible.

docs.title: Stage 2 — Fine-tuning GR00T on the Dataset
Base model
docs.text: nvidia/GR00T-N1.6-3B is the base checkpoint.

docs.title: Training command
docs.text:
CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 python \
    gr00t/experiment/launch_finetune.py \
    --base_model_path nvidia/GR00T-N1.6-3B \
    --dataset_path $DATASET_PATH \
    --modality_config_path examples/AddverbArm/addverb_config.py \
    --embodiment_tag NEW_EMBODIMENT \
    --num_gpus $NUM_GPUS \
    --output_dir /tmp/addverb_finetune \
    --save_steps 1000 \
    --save_total_limit 5 \
    --max_steps 10000 \
    --warmup_ratio 0.05 \
    --weight_decay 1e-5 \
    --learning_rate 1e-4 \
    --use_wandb \
    --global_batch_size 32 \
    --color_jitter_params brightness 0.3 contrast 0.4 saturation 0.5 hue 0.08 \
    --dataloader_num_workers 4

docs.title: Hyper-parameters
docs.text: Parameter Value Learning rate 1e-4 Global batch size 32 Max training steps 10 000 Warmup ratio 0.05 Weight decay 1e-5 Action horizon 16 Save every 1 000 steps

docs.title: Hardware
docs.text: Resource Requirement GPU 24 GB+ VRAM Training time 2–8 h (dataset-dependent) Inference target RTX 3080+ (real-time)

docs.title: Data requirements
docs.text: 50–200 demonstrations per task for basic competence, with language annotations and varied conditions.

docs.title: Required configuration
docs.text:
1. Dataset path. Point --dataset_path at the LeRobot root produced by Stage 1:
export DATASET_PATH=/home/vla-training/isaac_vla/General_Autonomy_Special_Robots/\
addverb_arms/addverb_vla_datacollection/addverb_single_arm_dataset

  1. Modality config. Author examples/AddverbArm/addverb_config.py describing:

  2. Joint count → 7 (6 active arm revolutes + 1 prismatic gripper; link_7 is welded as a fixed flange in sim and never enters the vector).

  3. Camera-name mapping that our parquet produces depends on the capture path: single-arm hardware emits camera1_images, camera2_images (top + wrist); the sim follower emits observation.images.ego and observation.images.external. Pick canonical names GR00T expects (e.g. top, wrist, or ego/external) and apply the rename via the config or via --rename_map at train time.

  4. Embodiment tag. --embodiment_tag NEW_EMBODIMENT is fine for a first run — GR00T will treat the Addverb arm as an unseen embodiment and fine-tune accordingly. Once you have a stable checkpoint, give it a stable tag (e.g. ADDVERB_7DOF) so co-trained datasets line up.

docs.title: Stage 3 — Sim Evaluation
docs.text:
Once a checkpoint exists, validate it inside the same Isaac Lab env it was trained against, with the same DR turned on, before risking the real arm.

docs.title: Policy server (Terminal 1, GPU host)
docs.text:
Same command as Stage 3:

python Isaac-GR00T/gr00t/eval/run_gr00t_server.py \
    --model-path /workspace/models/$MODEL

docs.title: Real-arm client (Terminal 2, on the data-collection PC)
docs.text:
Author addverb_eval.py under Isaac-GR00T/gr00t/eval/real_robot/Addverb/. Concretely it must:

  1. Drive the follower over its CAN interface (reuse the AddverbArmsJointController Python binding from addverb_arms/python_bindings/pybind/).
  2. Open three OpenCV cameras using the V4L2 paths from camera_config.json, capturing at 640×480 @ 30 fps: 
    cameras = {
    "top":   {"index_or_path": cam1_path, "width": 640, "height": 480, "fps": 30},
    "left":  {"index_or_path": cam2_path, "width": 640, "height": 480, "fps": 30},
    "right": {"index_or_path": cam3_path, "width": 640, "height": 480, "fps": 30},
}
  3. Connect to the GR00T server at --policy_host localhost --policy_port 5555.
  4. Pass --lang_instruction matching the trained prompt.
  5. Stream actions back to the arm at the same Hz the dataset was logged at (we use 30 Hz logging, while the underlying control loop runs at CONTROL_LOOP_RATE_HZ = 200 Hz; GR00T's action_horizon=16 chunk gives a smooth interpolation buffer).

docs.title: Success metrics
docs.text:
There is no automated success scorer in the eval pipeline. Plan to:

  • Run at least 20–30 rollouts per task so you can quote success rate with a useful confidence interval.
  • Have a human note success / failure / safety_intervention on each rollout in a CSV.

docs.title: Safety
docs.text:
The bridge already homes both arms on graceful shutdown (see _shutdown_sequence() in addverb_arms/addverb_vla_datacollection/data-collect.py). Reuse the same logic in addverb_eval.py. Add explicitly:

  • A workspace box check that rejects any commanded pose outside the table volume.
  • A physical e-stop wired to the leader controller.

docs.title: Stage 5 — Co-Training
docs.text: When real-only fine-tuning is unaffordable (we cannot collect 200 real demos per task) and sim-only DR has a residual sim-to-real gap, mix the two corpora.

docs.title: The principle
docs.text:
Source Quantity Quality Reality match Simulation Abundant Consistent Approximate Real teleop Limited Variable Exact

Each source plugs the other's hole.

docs.title: Recommended mixing ratio
docs.text:
Roughly 14 : 1 to 20 : 1 sim : real — a small set of real takes (≈5 episodes) paired with a much larger sim set (70–100). Concretely, for our Addverb pick-and-place:

  • Real: collect 5–10 good takes via run_datacollect_docker.sh (Stage 1, real path).
  • Sim: collect 70–100 takes with full DR via addverb_agent.py (Stage 1, sim path with DR turned on).

Apply the same DR ranges from §Stage 1 to the sim half — that is the whole point.

docs.title: Dataset merge step
docs.text:
%%{init: {'theme':'base','themeVariables':{'background':'transparent','primaryColor':'#f5f5f5','primaryBorderColor':'#383838','primaryTextColor':'#1f1f1f','lineColor':'#383838','textColor':'#1f1f1f','mainBkg':'#f5f5f5','nodeBkg':'#f5f5f5','nodeBorder':'#383838','clusterBkg':'#fafafa','clusterBorder':'#6b7280','titleColor':'#1f1f1f','edgeLabelBackground':'#ffffff','actorBkg':'#f5f5f5','actorBorder':'#383838','actorTextColor':'#1f1f1f','actorLineColor':'#6b7280','signalColor':'#1f1f1f','signalTextColor':'#1f1f1f','labelBoxBkgColor':'#f5f5f5','labelBoxBorderColor':'#383838','labelTextColor':'#1f1f1f','noteBorderColor':'#B45309','noteBkgColor':'#FEF3C7','noteTextColor':'#78350F'}}}%%
flowchart LR
    REAL[("addverb_real_dataset/<br/>5–10 takes, exact reality")]
    SIM[("addverb_sim_dataset/<br/>70–100 takes, full DR")]
    MERGE["Concatenate at parquet level<br/>re-number episode_index<br/>real first · sim second"]
    COT[("addverb_cotrain_dataset/<br/>14:1 → 20:1 sim:real")]
    TRAIN["Stage 2 GR00T command<br/>--dataset_path addverb_cotrain_dataset/"]

    REAL --> MERGE
    SIM --> MERGE
    MERGE --> COT --> TRAIN

    classDef real fill:#FCE7F3,stroke:#BE185D,color:#831843;
    classDef sim  fill:#DBEAFE,stroke:#1D4ED8,color:#1E3A8A;
    classDef cot  fill:#DCFCE7,stroke:#15803D,color:#14532D;
    class REAL real
    class SIM sim
    class COT cot

The default approach for Addverb:

  1. Produce two LeRobot dataset roots:
  2. addverb_real_dataset/ (Stage 1 real path)
  3. addverb_sim_dataset/ (Stage 1 sim path, full DR)
  4. Concatenate at the parquet level into a third root addverb_cotrain_dataset/ with episode_index re-numbered so that the real episodes appear first (operator can spot-check them easily) and sim episodes second.
  5. Train with the same Stage 2 GR00T command, pointing --dataset_path at addverb_cotrain_dataset/.
